docs.text: Action What it does Contents Paginated list of what's in this pouch. Eat Consume an edible item from this pouch. Drop Drop items from this pouch. Give Transfer items to another player. Move Move items from this pouch into your HUD personal inventory.

docs.title: Pouch action slot
docs.text: Each registered pouch gets an action slot (preferred slot 10, or the next free slot to the left). Touching that slot opens only that pouch — not your full HUD inventory.

docs.title: Physical vs character storage
docs.text: Unlike personal inventory, a worn pouch's contents travel with the pouch object, regardless of which character you're playing. Rez it on the ground and it acts like a chest; hand it to a friend and the contents go with it.

docs.title: How receive and spend work
docs.text:
When you receive items (gathering, crafting output, trades):

  1. Credit your HUD personal inventory first.
  2. If that's full, overflow into the first worn pouch with space (registry order).
  3. If there's still no space, the transfer fails — don't let pouches overflow silently.

When a station spends ingredients (recipes, deposits, etc.):

  1. Debit HUD personal inventory first.
  2. Then debit worn pouches in order until the recipe is satisfied.

You never choose which pouch pays at the station — the HUD aggregates everything you're carrying.

docs.title: Drink skins & water buckets
docs.text:
These are doorways to your personal inventory, not separate liquid tanks on the attachment.

Tool Typical slot Touch or slot opens What it handles Drink skin / waterskin / wineskin 8 Brewing liquids view (wine, must, mash, etc.) Forwards station liquid give/take into personal inventory Water bucket 9 Water view Wells and water sources fill water into personal inventory
  • Water recipes and wells use the bucket (or the water filter in inventory).
  • Brewing liquids (names ending in * in recipe data — you never type the *) route through the drink skin facade.
  • Wearing a registered skin or bucket prevents the HUD from double-counting liquids on world broadcasts.

Consumable vessels (mugs, horns, flasks)

Drinking horns, tankards, and similar props may get their own action-slot icon. They follow the same personal-inventory model on current HUD builds — use the slot or touch to drink from what's in your character inventory.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Keep personal inventory as your working set; use pouches for bulk or trade goods you'll hand off.
  • Use exact lowercase slugs in chests and menus.
  • If a craft says "not enough," the HUD counted everything — personal inventory and all worn pouches.
  • On regions still running legacy single-pouch gear, ask staff which products to wear.

Legacy pouches

Older Feudalism 3–style pouches that store items only in a legacy HUD list may not mix with the personal inventory model. Universes upgrading to Feudalism 4 should issue PI2 pouch products and the Personal Inventory HUD script set.

See also: Action Slots & Worn Tools · Storage Chests

docs.title: Storage Chests
docs.text: A storage chest is a rezzed container for bulk, shareable storage. Unlike your personal inventory, a chest's contents stay with the chest — and depending on its security, other players can use it too.

docs.title: Using a chest
docs.text:
Player steps

  1. Wear your F4 HUD (you don't strictly need a physical pouch — the HUD can act as your inventory).
  2. Touch the chest within range (you must be allowed by its security setting).
  3. Choose from the menu: Contents, Deposit, or Withdraw.
DepositWithdrawContents

Type the exact item slug and a quantity (or ALL) into the text box. The chest checks that you actually have that many, then moves them in.

Browse the paginated contents, pick an item, and build a quantity with +1 / +10 / +100 / All, then confirm. The items move into your inventory.

Just lists what's stored, with a search option for long lists.

Operations time out after about 12 seconds if your inventory doesn't respond — just try again.

docs.title: Capacity & security
docs.text:
• A chest holds items until its internal memory is full (it shows you roughly how much space is free).
  • The owner sets a security mode that controls who can use it: owner, group, or all.

docs.title: Character-agnostic by design
docs.text:
A chest doesn't care which character you are — it simply holds items. That's what makes chests great for:

  • Shared stockpiles for a group or shop,
  • Trading (deposit goods for a buyer to withdraw),
  • Moving items between your own characters (deposit as A, withdraw as B).

Pouches and personal inventory

On Feudalism 4 HUDs, personal inventory is character-bound on the HUD. Worn storage pouches (up to two) add capacity and can be rezzed or traded like chests — same idea as a chest, but worn on your avatar. See Personal Inventory & Pouches.

Match your inventory system

On regions still in transition, a chest may only see the inventory system you're actually using. If you deposit using the HUD but carry items only in a legacy pouch (or vice-versa), they might not line up — ask staff which system is live.

docs.title: For Item Owners
docs.text:
You bought (or built) Feudalism objects and want to deploy them: gatherables, ovens, fields, chests, targets, and more. This section covers configuring stations and writing recipes — no scripting required.

  • Setting Up Stations — the object description field, security modes, ranges, and per-type settings.
  • DropBox & Physical Delivery — central inventory for physical craft outputs (ovens, smiths).
  • Recipe Notecard Reference — every recipe/config notecard format, with real examples.

docs.title: Two places you'll configure things
docs.text: Where Used for Object Description Quick settings: the station's nouns/verbs, security (owner/group/all), special flags (fire, sit, debug). Notecard (recipes, config, deposits) The list of recipes or resources the station offers. DropBox (optional) Central stock of physical craft outputs when stations don't hold those objects locally.

docs.title: Physical delivery (DropBox)
docs.text: Recipes that hand out physical objects (plates, tools, hides) can pull from a DropBox on your estate instead of every station carrying a copy. See DropBox & Physical Delivery.

docs.title: The golden rules
docs.text:
1. Use exact lowercase item slugs everywhere — they're the contract between every object in your economy.
  2. Reset the script (or re-rez) after editing a notecard so changes load.
  3. Set security deliberately: owner (just you), group (your group), or all (anyone).
  4. Test each output type — a pouch item, a liquid, and a physical object behave differently.

Keep a master item list

The single most useful thing you can do is maintain one document of every item slug your universe uses. See Item Slug Conventions.

docs.title: DropBox & Physical Delivery
docs.text:
Use a DropBox when you want one central stock of physical crafting products (food plates, pots, tools, trophies) instead of hiding copies inside every oven or smith table. Crafters on any region can still receive those objects as long as they wear the Feudalism HUD and accept Experience permissions.

What stays the same

Recipes that output pouch items or liquids (drink skin / bucket) work exactly as before. DropBox only applies to physical objects handed to the player.

docs.title: How it works (owner view)
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  A[DropBox on your estate] --> B[Registers item types]
  C[Crafting station anywhere] --> D{Output in station?}
  D -- Yes --> E[Gives from station inventory]
  D -- No --> F[HUD delivers from DropBox]
  B --> F
  1. You keep master copies of physical items in a DropBox on your land.
  2. The DropBox registers those item names when it starts (and when inventory changes).
  3. A crafting station whose recipe names that object — but does not hold it locally — asks the crafter's HUD to deliver from the DropBox after a successful craft.
  4. If the same object is in the station inventory, the station gives it locally and ignores the DropBox.

Players do not interact with the DropBox directly.

docs.title: Setting up a DropBox
docs.text:
1. Rez a dedicated object (name it clearly, e.g. Feudalism 4 DropBox).
  2. Set Next Owner Copy: NO.
  3. Drop in all physical objects you want available for delivery.
  4. Add the F4 DropBox.lsl and F4 DropBox - Admin.lsl scripts (from your Feudalism 4 kit).
  5. Optional notecard dropbox.cfg:
heartbeat_minutes,30
  1. Reset the object.
  2. Accept Experience permissions.
  3. Confirm your parcel allows incoming HTTP (Linden URLs) for scripted objects.

Touch as owner to open the admin menu — use it to verify items registered and to test connectivity.

docs.title: Crafting stations that use DropBox
docs.text:
Station type Script stack (4 scripts per object) Oven, cauldron, fermenter F4 Crafting — Main, Recipes, Menu, Delivery Smith table, skinning station, multi-output bench F4 Crafting MultiItem — Main, Recipes, Menu, Delivery

Use the recipes notecard. Physical outputs must use the exact object name as it appears in the DropBox inventory.

  • Single-output format: Recipe Notecard Reference — Oven
  • Multi-output format: Recipe Notecard Reference — Smith table

Do not mix script families

Never put F4 Crafting and F4 Crafting MultiItem scripts on the same object. Never use the legacy Oven Script on new deployments.

docs.title: HUD requirement
docs.text:
Crafters must wear a current Feudalism 4 HUD that includes the delivery bridge scripts. If you sell or gift HUDs:

  • Update scripts before distribution.
  • Tell recipients to detach and re-wear after transfer and accept Experience again.

Without a current HUD, physical-output recipes fail at confirm with a clear error (not a silent failure).

docs.title: Naming checklist
docs.text: Check Why Recipe output name = DropBox object name Character-for-character match Lowercase slugs for pouch ingredients Consistent inventory checks Plates in DropBox: Next Owner Copy: No on the master Crafters receive no-copy plates One DropBox can register many item types Add/remove objects, then reset

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Problem What to try “Not registered for delivery” Object missing from DropBox; name typo; reset DropBox after inventory edit Delivery timeout Crafter HUD outdated; Experience not accepted; re-wear HUD Works for you, not visitors Gifted HUDs need script update + Experience re-accept Item delivered twice locally and from box Remove duplicate from station inventory if you want DropBox-only Registration count wrong Reset DropBox; check for duplicate object names; copy/clone needs reset Cloned DropBox steals delivery routes Reset the copy — it will not overwrite another box's registered items

docs.title: Recipe Notecard Reference
docs.text:
This is the complete reference for recipe and config notecards. Each station type reads a specific format — match it exactly. Lines beginning with # are comments; blank lines are ignored. Use exact lowercase item slugs.

Liquids carry a *

In recipe data, liquid items end with * (e.g. must*, mash*, water*). Liquids are taken from / given to the drink skin; solids use the pouch. Plain water routes to the skin too. Players never see the * in menus.

docs.title: Oven / cauldron / fermenter — notecard recipes
docs.text:
Scripts: F4 Crafting - Main, Recipes, Menu, Delivery (four scripts, same object).

Format

RecipeName,Category,Output,CR,ingredient1,qty1,ingredient2,qty2,...[,outputQty]
Field Meaning RecipeName Menu name and the pouch item produced (for pouch outputs). Category Menu grouping. Output pouch (virtual item), a liquid name like must* (to skin), or the exact name of a physical object (in the station or registered in a DropBox). CR Challenge Rating (≥ 0). Cook time = (CR + 1) × 20 s. ingredient,qty pairs What's consumed. outputQty (optional) Trailing integer = how many to produce.

Examples

stew,Soups,pouch,2,carrot,2,potato,1,onion,1,water,1
bread,Bakery,pouch,1,flour,2,yeast,1,water,1
Hearty Stew,Soups,Hearty Stew Plate v4.0,2,carrot,2,potato,2,water,1
must*,Wine,pouch,2,grapes,10
red wine,Wine,pouch,4,must*,1,water*,1,yeast,1
new red wine,Wine,pouch,2,must*,10,yeast,1,water,10,sugar,1,3
mash*,Mash,pouch,3,barley,4,water*,2,yeast,1
ale,Ale,pouch,3,mash*,1,hops,2,water*,1
mead,Mead,pouch,1,honey,3,yeast,1,water,2
  • Max 100 recipes.
  • XP: success (CR + 1) × 10, failure CR + 1.

Migrating Feudalism 3 pipe recipes

Convert |-delimited F3 lines to commas, e.g. Soup|1|Soup|physical|My Pot|1|carrot,2,water*,1 → Soup,Soup,My Pot,1,carrot,2,water*,1.

docs.title: Smith workstation (single output) — notecard recipes
docs.text:
Uses the same CSV as the oven. Install the F4 Crafting four-script stack (not legacy Smith Workstation scripts).

RecipeName,Category,Output,CR,ing1,qty1,...[,outputQty]

Examples

crushed cragsalt,ProcessedMineral,pouch,1,cragsalt,2
refined venom,Distillate,pouch,3,venom,3,water,1
lizard skin leather,CraftingMaterial,pouch,2,magic lizard,1,salt,1
crushed magestone dust,Reagent,pouch,3,red magestone,1,cragsalt,1,5
heart tissue slice,OrganicMatter,pouch,3,heart,1,salt,1,3
  • Trailing integer = outputQty (e.g. 5 dust, 3 slices).
  • Craft time (CR + 1) × 20 s; XP as the oven.
  • Keep RecipeName ≤ ~24 characters so it fits a menu button (the full name shows in the dialog body).
  • The station locks to one user until the craft finishes (~120 s session timeout).

docs.title: Smith table (multi-output) — notecard recipes
docs.text:
Install the F4 Crafting MultiItem four-script stack: MultiItem - Main, Recipes, Menu, Delivery.

Format

MenuName,Category,out1,qty1,dest1,out2,qty2,dest2,...,CR:#,DAMAGE:#,ing1,qty1,...[,TIME:seconds]
Part Meaning Output triplets item, qty, dest — repeat for each output. dest = pouch or exact object name CR:# Challenge rating (≥ 1). Time defaults to (CR+1)×20 s unless TIME: is set DAMAGE:# Optional health loss on failed craft roll Ingredients Same as oven — solids, water, liquids with *

Examples

# Pouch only
crushed cragsalt,ProcessedMineral,crushed cragsalt,1,pouch,CR:1,cragsalt,2

# Pouch + physical hide (DropBox or local)
butcher deer,Game,venison,2,pouch,deer pelt,1,Deer Pelt v4.0,CR:3,deer carcass,1,salt,1

# Timed weapon craft
forge blade,Weapons,iron blade,1,Iron Blade v4.0,CR:5,DAMAGE:2,iron ingot,2,coal,1,TIME:90
  • Each physical dest must match a local inventory object or a DropBox registration.
  • Use MultiItem Menu — do not install the single-item Menu script on the same object.

docs.title: Legacy smith (deprecated)
docs.text: Older Smith Workstation Recipes / pipe-format tables are replaced by the formats above. Do not deploy on new objects.

docs.title: Producer (press, churn, malting tub) — notecard recipes
docs.text:
One input → one output, no success roll.

outputItem,inputItem,qtyIn,qtyOut[,seconds[,xp[,pose[,bonusItem,bonusQty]]]]

Examples

must*,grapes,10,1,30,15
malt,barley,2,1,45,15
butter,cream,4,1,60,20,Churn
cream,milk,4,1,60,20,Churn,buttermilk,3
  • seconds defaults sensibly; xp defaults to 20.
  • pose is an optional animation name; bonusItem,bonusQty grants an extra byproduct.

docs.title: Farm field / herb pot / grape vine — notecard config
docs.text:
Plant → grow → harvest. Only the first line is used.

<recipeName>,<CR>,<qtyPerHarvest>,<ingredient1>,<qty1>,...

Examples

corn,3,1,seeds,2,water,1
wheat,1,2,wheat_seeds,1,water,1
parsley,2,5,parsleyseed,1,water,1,stover,1
grapes,2,1,grape_seeds,1,water,1
  • Grow time = CR × 100 s (5 s in debug).
  • XP per plant slot: success CR × 2, failure CR.
  • Harvest yields the recipeName crop (fields also give stover).

docs.title: Grain mill / grain silo — notecard config
docs.text:
Grind/dry grain into product in batches.

<outputItem>,<CR>,<yieldPerSuccess>,<ingredient1>,<qty1>,...

Examples

corn meal,1,2,corn seeds,1
flour,1,2,wheat berry,1
rye flour,1,2,rye seeds,1
fine flour,3,1,wheat berry,2
  • Processing time CR × 100 s per run; ~60 s cooldown afterward.

docs.title: Mine — notecard deposits
docs.text:
Category,DepositKey,ItemId,QtyPerGather,MaxPool,ResetSeconds,CooldownSeconds

Example

Metals,iron_vein,iron ore,2,40,600,3
Stone,granite,granite,1,30,900,4

docs.title: Feudalism 3 universal oven / weaponsmith table — *config* notecards
docs.text:
Legacy sit-based stations.

<Recipe Name>,<Category>,<Output Object Name>,<CR>,<ing1>,<qty1>,...

Beef Stew,Meals,Beef Stew,3,potato,2,carrot,1,beef,1,salt,1

Output is the exact inventory object name, or pouch for virtual items.

docs.title: Quick chooser
docs.text:
Station Notecard Key rule Oven / cauldron / fermenter recipes Name,Category,Output,CR,ing,qty… — F4 Crafting scripts Smith (single output) recipes same as oven — F4 Crafting scripts Smith table (multi-output) recipes …CR:#… output triplets — F4 Crafting MultiItem scripts Producer (press/churn) recipes out,in,qtyIn,qtyOut[,sec,xp…], no roll Field / herb pot / vine config name,CR,qty,ing,qty… (first line) Grain mill / silo config out,CR,yield,ing,qty… Mine deposits Category,Key,Item,qty,pool,reset,cooldown

After any notecard edit

Save it, then reset the station's script (or re-rez the object) so the new recipes load. Test one recipe of each output type.

docs.title: Setting Up Stations
docs.text:
Most Feudalism objects are configured through their Object Description (in the Edit window). Type the settings as comma-separated values. This page lists the description format for each common station type.

Security is almost universal

Nearly every object accepts a security token — owner, group, or all — controlling who may use it. Many also accept debug to print extra info to you while you set them up.

docs.title: Gatherables (bushes, fruit, wood, generic nodes)
docs.text:
<itemSlug>,<qty>[,<itemSlug>,<qty>...] ,<maxClicks>,<reset|die>,<resetSeconds>,<cooldownSeconds>[,security][,debug]

One click always grants every listed item (predefined amounts). Repeat item,qty pairs before the trail. The trail starts at the first field that is a number whose next field is reset or die (after the last qty you put maxClicks, then the action).

Field Notes reset / die Reset = reappears after the timer; die = destroyed when depleted. cooldown 1.0–30.0 seconds between clicks. reset time 1.0–3600.0 seconds.

Examples

banana,1,7,reset,300.0,3.0,all
iron ore,2,5,reset,600.0,2.0,group
rare herb,1,1,die,60,5.0,owner
log,2,5,reset,600.0,3.0,all
mulberry leaf,10,mulberry,10,6,reset,300,2,all

Range: 10 m. Coins (gold coin, etc.) cannot be gathered.

docs.title: Mining node (texture-changing)
docs.text:
<itemId>,<qtyPerClick>,<maxClicks>,<reset|die>,<resetSeconds>,<cooldown>[,security][,debug][,activeTexture][,depletedTexture]

iron ore,1,10,reset,600,3,all
copper ore,1,8,reset,900,4,group,,copper,rock

docs.title: Mine (multi-deposit)
docs.text:
• Description: mine,gather,gathering,all
  • Notecard named deposits (see the Recipe Notecard Reference).
  • Range: 15 m.

docs.title: Water well
docs.text:
<security>[,debug]

Touch within 5 m → Get Water fills a worn skin or bucket with water.

docs.title: Huntable animal
docs.text:
<animalName>,<health>,<resetDelaySec>,<meleeCR>,<security>[,debug]

deer,100,30,2,all
boar,150,60,3,group
wolf,80,20,4,all,debug
  • health — hit points; reset delay — respawn seconds (min 5).
  • melee CR — melee defense, bow difficulty base, and melee kill XP.
  • Loot: every object inside the animal drops on death.
  • Spawn points (owner): long-touch (~0.8 s) at up to 40 locations.

docs.title: Oven / cauldron / fermenter
docs.text:
Touch mode:

<noun>,<verb>,<verb-ing>,<ingredient-word>,<recipe-word>[,security][,fire]

oven,cook,cooking,ingredients,recipe,all

Sit mode — prefix with sit,:

sit,oven,cook,cooking,ingredients,recipe,all

Add fire to require a lit fire. Recipes go in a recipes notecard.

Scripts (four per object): F4 Crafting - Main, Recipes, Menu, Delivery. Do not use legacy Oven Script on new stations. See DropBox & physical delivery for central physical inventory.

docs.title: Food plate
docs.text:
<itemId>,<displayName>,<healthMod>,<staminaMod>,<maxServings>,<security>[,debug]

stew,Hearty Stew,5,3,12,all
bread,2,2,8,group

(The short form omits the display name.) Range: 5 m.

docs.title: Producer (press, churn, malting tub)
docs.text:
<mode>,<noun>,<verb>,<verb-ing>[,security][,debug]
  • mode = static (touch) or animated (AVsitter sit).
  • Recipes go in a recipes notecard (producer format).
static,press,press,pressing,all
animated,churn,churn,churning,group

docs.text:
• Description: <DisplayName>,<security> — e.g. Corn,all or Parsley,all.

fertilizer,all

owner

docs.title: DropBox (central physical inventory)
docs.text:
Dedicated object for physical crafting outputs used by stations that do not hold those objects locally.

  • Install F4 DropBox.lsl + F4 DropBox - Admin.lsl
  • Next Owner Copy: NO
  • Optional dropbox.cfg notecard
  • Accept Experience; allow parcel HTTP-in

Full setup: DropBox & physical delivery.

docs.title: Archery target / dummy
docs.text:
Practice target:

<name>,<baseCR>,<ringCount>,<marginPerRing>,<security>,<roleplay|arcade>

Practice Butt,3,5,10,all,roleplay

Practice dummy:

<name>,<health>,<resetDelay>,<meleeCR>,<security>

Practice Dummy,100,60,4,all

Managed ranges use a controller plus numbered firing pads — see your range builder guide.

Always reset after editing

Description changes usually apply on edit, but notecard changes require a script reset or re-rez.

docs.title: Recipes
docs.text:
Recipes are the heart of Feudalism crafting. This short section covers both sides:

  • Using Recipes

    For players — how recipes are presented, checked, and consumed at any station.

  • Authoring New Recipes

    For item owners — how to add your own recipes, in plain language, with the full format reference one click away.

Deep format reference

The exact, line-by-line notecard formats for every station type live in the Recipe Notecard Reference.

docs.title: Authoring New Recipes
docs.text:
If you own Feudalism stations, you can add your own recipes by editing a notecard inside the object. No scripting required — recipes are plain text, one per line.

This page is the friendly overview. For every format and field, see the Recipe Notecard Reference.

docs.title: The big idea
docs.text:
Most stations read a notecard (commonly named recipes or config) where each line is one recipe, written as comma-separated values:

RecipeName,Category,Output,CR,ingredient1,qty1,ingredient2,qty2,...

Reading the example below:

bread,Bakery,pouch,1,flour,2,yeast,1,water,1

Make bread (category Bakery), output to the player's pouch, difficulty CR 1, using 2 flour, 1 yeast, 1 water.

docs.title: The five things every recipe needs
docs.text:
1. A name — what players see and receive (keep it short for menu buttons; lowercase to match item slugs).
  2. A category — groups the recipe in the menu.
  3. An output — usually pouch (a virtual item named after the recipe), or the exact name of a physical object in the station's contents.
  4. A Challenge Rating (CR) — difficulty; also sets craft time and XP.
  5. Ingredients — pairs of item,quantity, using exact lowercase slugs.

docs.title: Liquids
docs.text: If an ingredient or output is a liquid (water, must, mash, wine…), its name ends with * in the data (e.g. must*). Liquids are taken from / given to the drink skin, while solids use the pouch.

docs.title: How to add a recipe — step by step
docs.text:
Owner steps

  1. Edit the station (you must own it) and open its recipes (or config) notecard.
  2. Add a line following the format for that station type.
  3. Use exact lowercase slugs that match your economy's item names.
  4. For physical outputs, drop the master object into the station's contents and set its Next Owner permissions appropriately (often Copy: No on plates/pots).
  5. Save the notecard, then reset the station's script (or re-rez it) so it reloads.
  6. Test one recipe of each output type (pouch item, liquid, physical object).

docs.title: Common pitfalls
docs.text:
• Misspelled slugs — iron ore ≠ iron_ore ≠ Iron Ore. Match exactly, lowercase.
  • Forgot to reset — notecard changes don't apply until the script reloads.
  • Wrong CR rules — some stations require CR ≥ 1 (you can't use CR 0 there).
  • Liquid mix-ups — don't forget the * on liquid names where required.

Slugs are your economy's contract

Every station that produces or consumes an item must agree on the same slug. Decide your item names once and keep a master list — see Item Slug Conventions.

Ready for specifics? → Recipe Notecard Reference

docs.title: Using Recipes
docs.text: As a player, you don't need to memorize anything — stations present their recipes to you. Here's what to expect and how to read the prompts.

docs.title: How a recipe is presented
docs.text:
1. Open the station (touch or sit).
  2. You see a category menu (e.g. Soups, Bakery, Wine). Pick one.
  3. You see the recipes in that category. Pick one.
  4. The station shows a Proceed? confirmation with what you're about to make.

docs.title: What the station checks
docs.text:
When you choose a recipe, the station looks for every ingredient it needs across your inventory and worn containers:

  • Solid ingredients come from your pouch.
  • Liquid ingredients (like water) come from your drink skin or bucket.

If you're short on anything, the station tells you what's missing and stops — nothing is taken.

docs.title: What happens when you proceed
docs.text:
Step What you'll see Consume Ingredients are removed from your inventory immediately. Timer A wait while the item is produced (longer for higher-CR recipes). Roll Your Crafting is rolled against the recipe's difficulty (on stations that roll). Result Success delivers the output; failure spends the ingredients for a little XP.

See How Crafting Works for the full breakdown.

docs.title: Reading recipe requirements
docs.text:
If your universe publishes its recipe list, you'll see lines that look like this (a cooking example):

stew,Soups,pouch,2,carrot,2,potato,1,onion,1,water,1

You can read that left to right as:

stew (in the Soups category) — needs Crafting/CR 2 — uses 2 carrot, 1 potato, 1 onion, 1 water.

You don't type these — the menu builds them for you — but knowing the shape helps you stock the right ingredients ahead of time.

Stock for the recipe, not the click

Because failures still consume ingredients, bring extra of anything for a high-CR recipe, and raise your Crafting stat to fail less often.

Owners who want to add recipes: see Authoring New Recipes.

docs.title: Reference
docs.text:
Quick lookups and answers.

  • Glossary — the terms you'll see in this guide and in-world.
  • FAQ & Troubleshooting — common questions and quick fixes.
  • Item Slug Conventions — how item names work and why they matter.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text:
I wore the HUD but nothing happens.

Make sure it's attached, not rezzed on the ground, and that you accepted the Experience permission. If you missed the popup, detach and re-wear. On first wear in the default universe, a starter character is usually created for you within a few seconds — try gathering or opening Inventory. Open Options (Setup HUD) to customize or create a character manually.

Do I have to finish character creation before I play?

No on current builds with auto-provision: a starter character is created so you can play right away. Open Setup when you want to change name, species, class, and stats. Code-gated universes may still require manual creation — ask staff.

Do I need a pouch?

For most gathering and crafting, yes — wear at least one storage pouch (up to two on Feudalism 4 personal-inventory HUDs). Your HUD personal inventory holds character-bound items; pouches add extra capacity and can be traded or rezzed like chests. A few things (food plates, some chest UIs) work with just the HUD.

How many pouches can I wear?

Two storage pouches on current Feudalism 4 builds. Attach a third and it should detach automatically. Label each pouch with its Description field so action slots and chat messages name the right bag.

docs.title: Saving & progression
docs.text:
My XP or stats reset / didn't save.

Almost always the Experience permission. Re-wear the HUD and accept it. Touch Sync/Update to force a save, and again after changing regions.

How many XP for a stat point?

1,000 XP = 1 Available Point. Partial thousands don't count until the next full 1,000. Raising a stat costs more at each step (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, …). See XP & Available Points.

Why is my mana bar empty?

Your character probably doesn't have mana — it's decided at creation by species and universe settings. That's normal for many builds.

docs.title: Crafting
docs.text:
The oven says I'm missing an ingredient I have.

Check the exact lowercase slug and quantity (iron ore, not Iron Ore or iron_ore). The HUD totals personal inventory and every worn pouch. Water should be in personal inventory (bucket slot or water filter); brewing liquids use the drink-skin path. On legacy regions, items may sit in an old pouch list that doesn't aggregate — ask staff.

I lost my ingredients on a failed craft.

That's expected — failures still consume ingredients (you get a little XP). Raise your Crafting stat to fail less, and bring spares for high-CR recipes.

The station won't open.

It may require you to sit, or need a lit fire (ovens), or its security may exclude you (owner/group only). The station usually tells you which.

Physical recipe fails at confirm or times out.

Wear your HUD, accept Experience permissions (detach and re-wear after a HUD transfer), and ensure the output object is in the station or registered in the estate DropBox with the exact name. Owners: DropBox & physical delivery.

docs.title: Inventory & storage
docs.text:
Where did my gathered items go?

Into your HUD personal inventory first. Open the Inventory button on the HUD, or a pouch's Contents menu, to see them. If personal inventory is full, overflow goes to a worn pouch. Use To Pouch / Move to shift items between HUD and pouches, or deposit into a storage chest.

Can another character see my items?

Your personal inventory is per-character — switch characters and you see that character's items. Chests and worn pouches are character-agnostic, so they're how you move goods between characters or trade with others.

docs.title: Combat
docs.text:
My sword swings but nothing happens.

Make sure you drew the weapon from the HUD slot, you're within its range, and you're targeting a valid combatant under your universe's consent rules.

My arrows don't register.

The arrow object's name must contain arrow (and not barrow). On managed ranges, stand on the lane firing pad first.

docs.title: Still stuck?
docs.text:
Contact your universe's staff with:

  • the object's name,
  • what you clicked, and
  • whether you're wearing F3 or F4 gear.

That's usually enough for them to diagnose it fast.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Term Meaning Action slot One of the 9 quick-use buttons on the HUD (slots 2–10) that link to worn tools (pouch, bucket, skin). Available Points Points spent on the Stats tab to raise attributes. 1,000 XP = 1 point. Challenge Rating (CR) A station's difficulty number; drives craft time, success rolls, and XP. Crafting The attribute rolled at most crafting and harvesting stations. Drink skin Worn doorway for brewing liquids (wine, must, …) stored in personal inventory; preferred action slot 8. Experience (SL) Second Life's permission/storage system the game uses to save your data. Accept the popup. Fire Some ovens require a lit fire nearby before they'll cook. Gatherable A touch-to-collect resource node (bush, ore, tree). HUD The worn attachment that runs your character — health, stamina, mana, XP, combat. Liquid (*) In recipe data, liquids end with * and route through the drink skin/bucket; players never see the *. Meter Optional attachment showing your floating name, title, and bars. Mode Roleplay, Tournament, OOC, AFK, or ★ — changes how you're treated in play. Personal inventory Your character's items on the HUD (pinv_*). Tied to the character, not the avatar. PI2 pouch A storage pouch whose items live on the attachment; up to two may be worn; contents travel with the pouch. Pouch A worn container for items; on F4 builds, storage pouches expand capacity and rezzed act like chests. Producer A simple station (press, churn, malting tub) with no success roll. Setup HUD The web panel for creating/editing your character (Options button). A starter character may be auto-created on first wear. Slot A unit of carrying capacity. (For now, one item type = one slot.) Slug The lowercase internal name of an item, e.g. iron ore. Stover Plant waste from harvesting; feeds compost. Sync / Update The HUD button that saves your XP and pools and refreshes character data. Universe A world (region or group) with its own characters, rules, species, classes, and economy. Vitals Health, Stamina, and Mana — your three pools. Water bucket Worn doorway for water in personal inventory; preferred action slot 9; used at wells. XP Experience earned by playing; converts to Available Points.

docs.title: Item Slug Conventions
docs.text: A slug is the internal name of an item. The entire economy — gatherables, recipes, pouches, chests — agrees on these names, so getting them right matters.

docs.title: The rules
docs.text:
1. Lowercase. iron ore, not Iron Ore.
  2. Spaces are fine. wine grapes, corn meal, red magestone are all valid slugs.
  3. Be consistent. Pick one spelling and reuse it everywhere. wheat berry and wheat_berry are different items to the system.
  4. Liquids end with * in recipe data (e.g. must*, water*, wine*). Players never see the *; it just tells the system to use the drink skin instead of the pouch.
  5. Coins are reserved. gold coin, silver coin, and copper coin can't be gathered or crafted — they flow only through the currency system.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text:
A single mismatch breaks a chain silently:

graph LR
  A[Gatherable yields wheat] --> B[Mill expects wheat berry]
  B --> X[No match - recipe fails]

If your gatherable produces wheat but your mill recipe asks for wheat berry, players gather a useless item. One canonical list prevents this.

docs.title: Keep a master list
docs.text:
Maintain a single document (a notecard or spreadsheet) of every slug your universe uses, grouped by chain. For example:

Chain Slugs Bread wheat → wheat berry → flour → bread Wine grapes → must* → wine Ale barley → malt → mash* → ale Metal iron ore → (ingot) → (tools/weapons) Dairy milk → cream → butter Compost stover/scraps/straw/manure/slop/eggshells → fertilizer

When you add a new product, add its slug to the list first, then wire up the gatherable and recipes to match.

Short names craft better

Recipe names double as menu buttons on some stations, which have limited button width. Keep produced item names reasonably short (the full name still shows in the confirmation dialog).
